folio.label: 142r
folio.l: Oure hoost bygan to swere , as he were wood

note.notetxt: 1 The prologue begins without incipit or an indication to its inclusion on f. 142r. It appears at the end of PH, inserted above the bottom ruling line of the frame.ODR

folio.l: Harrowe q¶ he , by nayles and by blood
This was a fals cherl , a fals Iustise
As shameful deetú , as herte kan deuyse
Come to theise Iuges , and here Aduocates
Algate this sely mayde , is slayn , allas
Allas , to dedrederededre a bougút she hire beaute
Wherfore I seye alday , that men moun se
That ©iftes of fortune , and of nature
Ben cause of deetú , to many a creature
Of bothe ©iftes , that I speke of now
Men han ful ofte , more for harm than prow
¢ But trewely , myn owyn Maister deere
This is a pitous tale , for to heere
But nathelees , passe ouä , is no fors
I preye to god , so saue thy gentil cors
And eke thyne vrynals , and thyne Iurdones
Thyn Ypocras , and eke thy Galiones
And euäy boiste , ful of thy letuarie
God blisse he~ , and oure lady Seynt Marie
So mote I then , thow art a propre man
And lyke a prelat , by Seynt Ronyan
Seyde I nat wel , I kan nat speke in täme
But wel I woot , thow doost myn herte to erme
That I almost haue caugút , a Cardynacle
By Corpus bones , but I haue tryacle
Or elles a draugút of moiste , and corny ale
Or but I heere a noon , a murie tale
Myn herte is loste , for pitee of this mayde
Thow beal amy , thow Pardoner he sayde
Telle vs sum myrthe , of Iapes rigút a noon
¢ It shal be don q¶ he , by Seynt Ronyon
But first q¶ he , heere at this Ale stake
I wol bothe drynke and eten , of a Cake
But rigút a noon , theise gentils gonne to crie
Nay lat hi~ nat tl tl telle vs , of no ribaudie
Telle vs som moral thyngö , that we may lere
Som wyt , and thanne wol we gladly heere
I graunte I wys q¶ he , but I must thynke
Vp on som honest thyngö , whil that´ I drynke
